pi_items.source: Verified via precedence rule: 'Code A (Acid): If Reactive Group contains Acids' - actual group 'Acids, Strong Non-oxidizing' contains 'Acids'
Verified via precedence rule: 'Code O (Oxidizer): If Reactive Group contains Oxidizing Agents' - actual group 'Oxidizing Agents, Strong' contains 'Oxidizing Agents'
Verified via precedence rule: 'Code B (Base): If Reactive Group contains Bases OR Amines' - actual group 'Amines, Phosphines, and Pyridines' contains 'Amines'
Verified via precedence rule: 'Code F (Flammable): If Reactive Group contains Ketones, Aldehydes, Esters, or Hydrocarbons' - actual group 'Ketones' contains 'Ketones'
Verified via precedence rule: 'Code B (Base): If Reactive Group contains Bases OR Amines' - actual group 'Bases, Strong' contains 'Bases'

privileged_info_summary: All five chemicals have been verified against the official NOAA CAMEO Chemicals Database. Hydrochloric Acid belongs to 'Acids, Strong Non-oxidizing' reactive group and should be assigned code A. Sodium Permanganate belongs to 'Oxidizing Agents, Strong' and should be assigned code O. Triethylamine belongs to 'Amines, Phosphines, and Pyridines' and should be assigned code B. Methyl Ethyl Ketone belongs to 'Ketones' and should be assigned code F. Potassium Hydroxide belongs to 'Bases, Strong' and should be assigned code B. All expected storage codes are correctly assigned according to the specified precedence rules.
